article_meta.title: President Donald Trump’s mass deportation operation is expected to ramp up in 2026 as newly hired military lawyers-turne

reasoning: Mostly neutral summary that includes both supportive and critical perspectives. Minor adoption of the article’s loaded phrasing (e.g., “mass deportation operation”) and a slightly interpretive claim (“reshape the judiciary”) without attribution prevent a top score.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses one interpretive phrase (“reshape the judiciary”) and adopts “mass deportation operation” as factual.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: “Reshape the judiciary” is asserted without direct attribution; most other claims mirror article data.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Treats “mass deportation operation” as an objective descriptor; otherwise attributes critiques to sources.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Mildly elevated terms like “mass deportations,” but overall measured tone.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives beyond sourced quotes.
custom_reasoning.slant: Includes both proponents’ and critics’ views with roughly balanced emphasis.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Captures key perspectives (administration/supporters, data sources, critics); omits some specifics (e.g., denial rates) but not a perspective.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Limited use; “mass” in this context is loaded but drawn from article.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically charged term “mass deportation” repeated without distancing; otherwise neutral vocabulary.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Notes constraints on appeals and due process concerns but also mentions backlog relief arguments.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential deference to elites or grassroots beyond source content.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than source but still inherits a few loaded framings.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Lists major sources/perspectives present; broadly proportional.
